2604.14140.title: LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
2604.14140.paper_summary: As language models are increasingly deployed for complex autonomous tasks, their ability to reason accurately over longer horizons becomes critical. An essential component of this ability is planning and managing a long, complex chain-of-thought (CoT). We introduce LongCoT, a scalable benchmark of 2,500 expert-designed problems spanning chemistry, mathematics, computer science, chess, and logic to isolate and directly measure the long-horizon CoT reasoning capabilities of frontier models. Problems consist of a short input with a verifiable answer; solving them requires navigating a graph of interdependent steps that span tens to hundreds of thousands of reasoning tokens. Each local step is individually tractable for frontier models, so failures reflect long-horizon reasoning limitations. At release, the best models achieve <10% accuracy (GPT 5.2: 9.8%; Gemini 3 Pro: 6.1%) on LongCoT, revealing a substantial gap in current capabilities. Overall, LongCoT provides a rigorous measure of long-horizon reasoning, tracking the ability of frontier models to reason reliably over extended periods.
2604.14140.paper_summary_zh: 隨著語言模型越來越多地被用於複雜的自主任務，它們在較長時間範圍內準確推理的能力變得至關重要。這種能力的一個基本組成部分是規劃和管理一個長而複雜的思維鏈（CoT）。我們推出了 LongCoT，一個可擴展的基準，包含 2,500 個專家設計的問題，涵蓋化學、數學、計算機科學、棋類和邏輯，以孤立並直接測量前沿模型的長時間範圍 CoT 推理能力。問題由一個短輸入和一個可驗證的答案組成；解決這些問題需要在一個跨越數十到數十萬個推理標記的相互依賴步驟的圖中導航。每個局部步驟對於前沿模型來說都是可單獨處理的，因此失敗反映了長時間範圍推理的限制。在發布時，最佳模型在 LongCoT 上的準確率低於 10%（GPT 5.2: 9.8%；Gemini 3 Pro: 6.1%），顯示出當前能力的顯著差距。總體而言，LongCoT 提供了一個嚴格的長時間範圍推理測量，追蹤前沿模型在延長時間內可靠推理的能力。
2604.14140.author: Sumeet Ramesh Motwani et.al.
2604.14140.authors: Sumeet Ramesh Motwani, Daniel Nichols, Charles London, Peggy Li, Fabio Pizzati, Acer Blake, Hasan Hammoud, Tavish McDonald, Akshat Naik, Alesia Ivanova, Vignesh Baskaran, Ivan Laptev, Ruben Glatt, Tal Ben-Nun, Philip Torr, Natasha Jaques, Ameya Prabhu, Brian Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Christian Schroeder de Witt
